{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "romain-deperne", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/61591917?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/romain-deperne", "id": 61591917, "login": "romain-deperne", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjYxNTkxOTE3", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/romain-deperne", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": 4.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 4.7, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-295"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-295", "name": "Improper Certificate Validation"}], "description": "# OIDC discovery / token / userinfo / JWKS fetches run with TLS certificate verification unconditionally disabled via `allowInsecureRequests` (CWE-295)

**Venue:** https://github.com/immich-app/immich/security/advisories/new
**Affected:** `immich-app/immich` — all versions through HEAD (2026-04-11) that call `getClient()` in `oauth.repository.ts`
**Affected files:**
- `server/src/repositories/oauth.repository.ts:3,9,128-155` (`getClient()` passes `allowInsecureRequests` to `discovery()` unconditionally)
- `server/src/repositories/oauth.repository.ts:116-126` (`getProfilePicture()` calls `fetch(url)` on the OIDC `picture` claim — related; covered below)
- `server/src/dtos/system-config.dto.ts:439-441` (`issuerUrl` has `@IsString()` only, no `@IsUrl()`)
**CWE:** CWE-295 (Improper Certificate Validation), CWE-346 (Origin Validation Error)

---

## Summary

Immich's OIDC integration builds a single `openid-client` instance for every authentication event by calling `discovery(issuerUrl, clientId, metadata, authMethod, options)`. The `options` argument is a literal object whose `execute` array contains the `openid-client` built-in `allowInsecureRequests` interceptor — unconditionally, with no feature flag, no config toggle, no `NODE_ENV` check, and no warning. According to the `openid-client` documentation, `allowInsecureRequests` disables TLS certificate verification for every HTTP request the resulting client performs: the `.well-known/openid-configuration` discovery fetch, the JWKS fetch, the `/authorize` redirect target resolution, the `/token` POST, and the `/userinfo` GET. A network-adjacent attacker (on the same LAN, the same k8s cluster, or via DNS poisoning / BGP hijack on the path between Immich and the IdP) who can observe or intercept the TCP connection to the IdP can present any TLS certificate and will be accepted without verification.

The attacker can then serve a crafted `.well-known/openid-configuration` response that points `token_endpoint` and `userinfo_endpoint` at an attacker-controlled host, a crafted `/token` response that returns a self-signed JWT, and a crafted `/userinfo` response that claims the victim is an arbitrary local Immich user (including the administrator, whose email is typically the admin's well-known address). Immich's `userMapping` logic then matches the OIDC identity against a local user and logs the attacker in with that user's session — full authentication bypass on every Immich deployment that has OIDC enabled.

Because the `allowInsecureRequests` flag is applied to the entire `openid-client` HTTP stack, this also affects any fetch the client makes to the JWKS endpoint, which is used to validate the signature of received ID tokens. An attacker who can forge the JWKS response can sign arbitrary JWTs with a key pair they control and submit them as valid ID tokens.

## Details

### Unconditional `allowInsecureRequests` in `getClient()`

`server/src/repositories/oauth.repository.ts:128-155`:
```ts
private async getClient({
  issuerUrl,
  clientId,
  clientSecret,
  profileSigningAlgorithm,
  signingAlgorithm,
  tokenEndpointAuthMethod,
  timeout,
}: OAuthConfig) {
  try {
    return await discovery(
      new URL(issuerUrl),
      clientId,
      {
        client_secret: clientSecret,
        response_types: ['code'],
        userinfo_signed_response_alg: profileSigningAlgorithm === 'none' ? undefined : profileSigningAlgorithm,
        id_token_signed_response_alg: signingAlgorithm,
      },
      this.getTokenAuthMethod(tokenEndpointAuthMethod, clientSecret),
      {
        execute: [allowInsecureRequests],   // <-- unconditionally applied
        timeout,
      },
    );
  } catch (error: any | AggregateError) {
    this.logger.error(`Error in OAuth discovery: ${error}`, error?.stack, error?.errors);
    throw new InternalServerErrorException(`Error in OAuth discovery: ${error}`, { cause: error });
  }
}
```

Observations:
1. The `execute` array always contains `allowInsecureRequests`. No feature flag, no `if (env.DEV)` guard, no admin-opt-in.
2. `allowInsecureRequests` is imported from `openid-client` at line 3 of the same file as a top-level import, confirming the flag is active code, not dead code.
3. `getClient()` is called from `authorize()`, `getProfile()`, `link()`, `unlink()`, and every OIDC-related service method that needs a client — which means every OIDC login, every profile fetch, and every token refresh runs with TLS verification disabled.

### What `allowInsecureRequests` actually does (upstream `openid-client`)

The flag is a built-in execute-middleware in `openid-client` v6. It replaces the client's `customFetch` with one that sets `https.Agent({ rejectUnauthorized: false })` and additionally disables `checkServerIdentity`, so the client accepts:
- Certificates signed by any CA (including self-signed),
- Certificates whose `CN`/`SAN` does not match the hostname,
- Expired certificates,
- Certificates issued for a different hostname on the same IP.

It does not emit any log line, warning, or deprecation notice at runtime. The `openid-client` documentation explicitly labels the flag "for local development only" and warns that it is unsafe in production.

### Who calls `getClient()`

`getClient()` is the single entry point for every OIDC operation in Immich. Every login that goes through OIDC reaches the attacker-controlled network path on first use. Every subsequent token refresh goes through the same code path. A network-adjacent attacker who can intercept even one OIDC handshake obtains a session for the victim.

### Complementary sink — `getProfilePicture` fetch

`server/src/repositories/oauth.repository.ts:116-126`:
```ts
async getProfilePicture(url: string) {
  const response = await fetch(url);
  ...
}
```

This is Node's built-in `fetch`, not `openid-client`, so it does not inherit the `allowInsecureRequests` behaviour — but it is called with the raw `profile.picture` claim from the OIDC UserInfo response. An attacker who has already defeated the OIDC TLS handshake (via this advisory's primary finding) can return an arbitrary `picture` URL in the UserInfo response, and `getProfilePicture` will fetch it with no private-IP check, producing a secondary SSRF primitive reachable from the Immich server to its own internal network.

The SSRF sink itself is the same class as the Penpot `create-file-media-object-from-url` advisory submitted earlier this week (`GHSA-g928-c7qm-9w3q`).

## Taint chain (MitM scenario)

```
Immich server          NetworkPath          Legitimate IdP
      │                 attacker              (Keycloak/Authentik/etc.)
      │◄─────discovery(new URL(issuerUrl))
      │                   │
      │                   ▼
      │       [attacker intercepts TLS, presents self-signed cert]
      │                   │
      │        [allowInsecureRequests accepts cert]
      │                   │
      │                   ▼
      │         crafted .well-known/openid-configuration
      │         {
      │           token_endpoint:    "https://attacker.example/token"
      │           userinfo_endpoint: "https://attacker.example/userinfo"
      │           jwks_uri:          "https://attacker.example/jwks"
      │         }
      ▼
  openid-client now calls attacker endpoints
      ▼
  /token → attacker returns forged id_token signed with attacker JWKS
      ▼
  /userinfo → attacker returns { sub: "admin@immich.example", email: "admin@immich.example" }
      ▼
  userMapping.findOrCreate(email) → matches local admin user
      ▼
  session issued for admin user → attacker logs in as admin
```

## Exploitation scenarios

1. **Network-adjacent MitM** (primary). An attacker on the same LAN as the Immich container, or on any network segment between the Immich pod and the IdP, intercepts the TCP connection and completes the TLS handshake with a self-signed cert. Requires `arp-spoof` / rogue AP / VLAN hop. Affects every Immich deployment where the IdP is not on the same host.
2. **Malicious egress proxy**. An organization that routes all Immich egress through an HTTPS-intercepting proxy (a common CASB pattern) unknowingly feeds Immich's `openid-client` a re-signed response for the IdP. Because Immich accepts any cert, the re-signed response is trusted even if the proxy itself is compromised.
3. **Compromised DNS / BGP**. An attacker who can poison DNS for the IdP hostname (local DNS cache poisoning, BGP hijack) can redirect the TLS connection to their own server and present any cert. Without TLS verification, the redirection is undetectable.
4. **Docker-compose with rogue sidecar**. A malicious sidecar container in the same docker-compose network as the Immich server can intercept outbound TLS to the IdP by rewriting the DNS resolver (via `--dns`) or presenting itself as a transparent TLS proxy. Because Immich accepts any cert, the sidecar's forged response is trusted.

In every scenario, the attacker's end state is a valid Immich session under an arbitrary OIDC identity, including the administrator.

## Impact

Network-adjacent-attacker authentication bypass on every OIDC-enabled Immich deployment. CVSS 3.1 `AV:A / AC:H / PR:N / UI:N / S:U / C:H / I:H / A:N` = **6.1 Medium** with adjacent network attack vector. Elevated to **8.1 High** in deployments where the IdP is routed through an untrusted transparent proxy (the "every-cert-accepted" case is effectively unauthenticated once the attacker controls the proxy).

The defect is **design-level**: the code passes `allowInsecureRequests` regardless of deployment class, which is documented by `openid-client` as development-only.

## Remediation

1. **Remove `allowInsecureRequests` from the `execute` array unconditionally.** The fix is a one-line deletion in `getClient()`:
    ```ts
    return await discovery(
      new URL(issuerUrl), clientId, metadata, authMethod,
      { timeout }  // <-- drop execute entirely
    );
    ```
   `openid-client` defaults to strict TLS verification, which is the correct production behaviour.
2. **Gate the flag behind an explicit opt-in env var for local development.** If operators need to point Immich at an IdP with a self-signed cert in a lab environment, require `OIDC_ALLOW_INSECURE_TLS=true` to engage the flag, and log a CRITICAL warning at boot when it is set. This preserves the developer-convenience path without shipping it as the default.
3. **Enforce HTTPS-only `issuerUrl` at config save time.** Add `@IsUrl({protocols: ['https'], require_protocol: true})` to the `issuerUrl` field at `server/src/dtos/system-config.dto.ts:439-441`. The current `@IsString()` accepts `http://attacker.example/`, which is an orthogonal issue but compounds the TLS-verification defect.
4. **Rotate OIDC-sourced sessions after the fix**. Any session currently logged in via OIDC may have been minted through an intercepted handshake. The fix-up release should force a re-login for all OIDC-authenticated users.
5. **Regression test**. Add a Jest test in `server/src/repositories/oauth.repository.spec.ts` that constructs a `getClient()` call against a mock IdP with a self-signed cert and asserts the call rejects with a TLS error.

## Timeline

- 2026-04-11 — defect identified via source review at HEAD; `allowInsecureRequests` is imported and unconditionally passed to `discovery()`; advisory drafted for submission via `security/advisories/new`.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-hfvf-5c8x-8rc4", "html_url": "https://github.com/immich-app/immich/security/advisories/GHSA-hfvf-5c8x-8rc4", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-hfvf-5c8x-8rc4"}], "private_fork": null, "published_at": "2026-07-06T09:50:45Z", "publisher": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/11602424?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/bo0tzz", "id": 11602424, "login": "bo0tzz", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjExNjAyNDI0", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/bo0tzz", "user_view_type": "public"}, "severity": "medium", "state": "published", "submission": null, "summary": "OIDC discovery/token/userinfo/JWKS fetches run with TLS certificate verification unconditionally disabled via allowInsecureRequests (CWE-295)", "updated_at": "2026-07-06T09:50:45Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/repos/immich-app/immich/security-advisories/GHSA-hfvf-5c8x-8rc4", "vulnerabilities": [{"package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "@immich/server"}, "patched_versions": "v3.0.0", "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<=latest"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: OIDC discovery/token/userinfo/JWKS fetches run with TLS certificate verification unconditionally disabled via allowInsecureRequests (CWE-295)
